facts: The beat is the steady pulse you can clap along to; in 'Twinkle Twinkle Little Star,' the beat stays the same speed even when the melody goes up and down.
Loud and soft are dynamics that describe how much sound energy a song uses; a lullaby is usually soft to help you relax, while a marching song is often loud to make you feel energetic.

Rhythm is the pattern of long and short sounds in a song, different from the steady beat; clapping the rhythm of 'Mary Had a Little Lamb' makes a pattern like 'long-short-short-long-long-long-short-short,' while clapping the beat is just a steady 'ta ta ta ta.'
misconceptions: The beat gets faster when the melody goes higher, and slower when the melody goes lower.
